mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million units signals a strengthening demand for residential construction, which in turn boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 104.5 signals a weakening supply chain, which in turn reduces inventory levels and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 104.5 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market, which in turn reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 147,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 4.5% signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods, which in turn boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 4.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the consumer price index above 2.5% signals a strengthening monetary policy, which in turn reduces inflation expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals a strengthening labor market, which in turn boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 4.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 4.0% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which in turn boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 4.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.
